constraints: The dry-run proof packet must include inbox, task-intake, calendar-conflict, draft-response, approval-request, no-send, memory-candidate, receipt-replay, and closure evidence.
The packet must remain digest-only and local.

Email send, calendar write, task write, mailbox mutation, connector mutation, memory write, memory admission, deployment mutation, customer-readiness claim, production-readiness claim, and live Nested Mind activation remain denied.
Foundation closure packet source digests must be regenerated after the dry-run packet fixture changes.
non_goals: Do not call Gmail or Calendar.
Do not create, move, cancel, or invite calendar events.
Do not admit or write memory.
Do not claim customer readiness or production readiness.
problem_statement: The existing Personal Assistant dry-run packet closed an inbox-to-draft proof, but the flagship dry-run needed explicit calendar-conflict reasoning, task-intake projection, no-send proof, and memory-admission candidate review without granting live authority.
success_criteria: Dry-run packet validates as SolvedVerified and closed.
Source artifact count is eleven and stage count is twelve.
Calendar-conflict, task-intake, draft-response, approval-request, no-send, and memory-candidate closure flags are true.
No-effect boundary explicitly denies send, calendar write, task write, mailbox mutation, connector mutation, memory write, and memory admission.
Foundation closure packet remains SolvedVerified after digest refresh.
